title: Detection of Sars-Cov2 Using Droplet Digital PCR
author: Joseph Patterson
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.be8tjhwn

steps.title: Add 11 µL of Master mix...
steps.instruction: Add 11 µL of Master mix to each PCR tube used. •4.4 µL iScript Reverse Transcription Supermix for RT-qPCR (Bio-Rad 1708841). •6.6 µL DNase/RNase free water.

steps.title: Add 11 µL of sample/diluted RNA...
steps.instruction: Add 11 µL of sample/diluted RNA to each tube (identify optimal dilution prior to performing the real experiment).

steps.title: Close tubes and shake by hand to mix
steps.instruction: Close tubes and shake by hand to mix.

steps.title: Centrifuge to collection samples at the bottom of the tubes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge to collection samples at the bottom of the tubes.

steps.title: Perform cDNA synthesis reaction in a Thermocycler (~30 min
steps.instruction: Perform cDNA synthesis reaction in a Thermocycler (~30 min. total). •Constant lid temperature of 105ºC. •5 min. at 25ºC. •20 min. at 46ºC. •1 min. 95ºC • Hold at 4ºC.

steps.title: Dispense 11 µL master mix to each PCR tube
steps.instruction: Dispense 11 µL master mix to each PCR tube. •9 parts ddPCR Supermix for Probes (no dUTP) (Bio-Rad 186-3026). •1 part Probe

steps.title: Transfer 11 µL of cDNA to...
steps.instruction: Transfer 11 µL of cDNA to each PCR tube containing master mix.

steps.title: Mix samples and centrifuge to collect...
steps.instruction: Mix samples and centrifuge to collect at the bottom of the tube.

steps.title: Obtain a DG8 droplet generator cartridge...
steps.instruction: Obtain a DG8 droplet generator cartridge (Bio-Rad 1864008) and transfer 20 µL of sample to the sample well.

steps.title: Transfer 70 µL of droplet generation...
steps.instruction: Transfer 70 µL of droplet generation oil (Bio-Rad 1863005) to the oil well.

steps.title: Secure the gasket (Bio-Rad 1863009) over...
steps.instruction: Secure the gasket (Bio-Rad 1863009) over the cartridge, and used the QX droplet generator (Bio-Rad 186-4002) to generate droplets.

steps.title: Transfer 40 µL droplets to a...
steps.instruction: Transfer 40 µL droplets to a 96 well plate (Bio-Rad 12001925).

steps.title: When all droplets for the experiment...
steps.instruction: When all droplets for the experiment have been transferred, cover plate with pierceable foil (Bio-Rad 181-4040) with the RED LINE SIDE UP AT THE TOP,

steps.title: Heat seal with a plate sealer (Bio-Rad 181-4000)
steps.instruction: Heat seal with a plate sealer (Bio-Rad 181-4000).

steps.title: Transfer plate to a Real-Time thermocycler
steps.instruction: Transfer plate to a Real-Time thermocycler. •10 min. at 95ºC •40 cycles (30 s 94ºC, 1 min. 60ºC) •10 min. at 98ºC. •Hold at 4ºC.

steps.title: Transfer plate to droplet reader and run
steps.instruction: Transfer plate to droplet reader and run.

steps.title: Analysis is performed with the Quantasoft...
steps.instruction: Analysis is performed with the Quantasoft software associated with the droplet reader.

steps.title: Lower limit of quantitation is 10 copies/ul
steps.instruction: Lower limit of quantitation is 10 copies/ul.

steps.title: Lower limit of detection is 1 copy/ul
steps.instruction: Lower limit of detection is 1 copy/ul.

steps.title: A single present copy/ul in both...
steps.instruction: A single present copy/ul in both N1 and N2 probes is classified as a suspected positive test.

steps.title: A human RNaseP probe is used...
steps.instruction: A human RNaseP probe is used to confirm a quality sample of RNA was obtained from each subject.

steps.title: A 500 copy/ul cDNA standard is...
steps.instruction: A 500 copy/ul cDNA standard is run with the N1 and N2 probes on each plate as a positive control.

steps.title: A human RNaseP, SARS-Cov2 N1, or...
steps.instruction: A human RNaseP, SARS-Cov2 N1, or SARS-Cov2 N2 probe is used in each reaction.

steps.title: In addition cDNA made from human...
steps.instruction: In addition cDNA made from human samples, a comercial cDNA control for SARS-Cov2 is also used with each probe as a control.
